page: Vultr voc-c-1c-2gb-25s-amd is an entry-level virtual server within the Optimized Cloud Compute family. It is equipped with a single shared AMD EPYC vCPU operating at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 2.0 GB of system memory, 25 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a single complimentary public IPv4 address. This instance does not include a GPU.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the top tier. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also show strong performance. However, due to the shared CPU allocation and single-core limitation, multi-core performance and PassMark physics tests are weak. Memory read bandwidth is low, though large block write bandwidth remains average. Database operations and Redis throughput are also limited by the resource constraints.

This server offers a cost-efficient profile for low-density workloads where dedicated CPU resources are not required. The primary tradeoffs are the shared CPU allocation and the small 2.0 GB memory capacity, which make it unsuitable for high-concurrency applications. It is best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks, basic utility scripting, software development environments, and low-traffic web serving.
description: Vultr voc-c-1c-2gb-25s-amd is an entry-level virtual server in the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring a single shared AMD EPYC vCPU at 2.0 GHz, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 25 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks and strong single-threaded compression speeds. However, its shared CPU allocation and limited memory capacity result in weak multi-core and database performance. This budget-friendly configuration is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, basic scripting, and low-traffic development environments.
